summary: The checkout is at the same commit as origin/main (545dec476), and no recent commit references #2557. Existing direct-Codex controls, Omnigent bridge events/elicitation support, durable session observability, and passive Live Logs satisfy portions of the issue baseline. The canonical runtime-neutral request/result/capability contract and durable idempotent command path are absent: the current ArtifactSessionControlRequest contains only action, message, and reason, and its response contains only action plus a refreshed projection. Workflow Chat also lacks the complete Omnigent message/elicitation/interrupt/stop/terminal control experience. New code work is required.

requirements.description: Workflow Chat exposes only controls supported by the current host/session, MoonMind policy, and operator authorization. Existing Workflow Detail controls consume server-projected intervention capabilities, but the projection is limited to the direct compatibility surface and is not the issue’s complete host/session/policy/approval/authorization resolver.

Every control has a stable request ID, idempotency key, actor, target session/turn precondition, reason where required, and durable result. The current ArtifactSessionControlRequest has only action, message, and optional reason; ArtifactSessionControlResponse has only action and projection.

Duplicate submissions cannot duplicate the provider side effect. The current artifact-session control endpoint accepts no request identity or idempotency key and dispatches a Temporal update directly.
Stale epoch/session/turn requests fail closed with a stable reason. The current public control request accepts no expected epoch/session version/turn ID and the response has no stable reason code.

Omnigent message, elicitation response, interrupt, and stop flows have end-to-end API/UI coverage. Bridge event and elicitation routes plus conformance tests exist, while the complete typed control API and Workflow Chat UI journey does not.

Unsupported clear/reset returns an explicit create-new-session policy rather than pretending the host was cleared. Omnigent bridge design and bridge-local behavior represent unsupported clear/reset as requiring a new session.

Existing direct Codex controls use the same request/result/capability contract behind a compatibility gate. Direct controls exist, but no shared canonical request/result contract with the Omnigent path exists.
continue_same_session, checkpoint Resume, and full_retry remain distinct throughout API and UI. Current workflow detail schemas and recovery UI model these as separate actions.

Control outcomes appear in Workflow Chat and durable audit evidence after reload and after the live host is gone. Durable session/control observability exists for the direct path, but the canonical control receipt and complete Workflow Chat outcome projection are absent.

Authorization, policy denial, concurrent control, timeout/reconciliation, terminal-session, and provider-rejection journeys are tested. Authorization and terminal/capability checks have coverage, but the canonical concurrent-control and delivery-unknown reconciliation journeys cannot exist without the missing command contract.

Live Logs remains passive observation; no control depends on an SSE connection or browser-local state. Canonical Live Logs documentation and the current separation of the artifact-session control endpoint from log streaming satisfy this requirement.
